title: Pre-operative anesthesia assessment
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports previous-anesthesia history, medical therapy, focused examination, airway and physical-condition assessment, and factors affecting perioperative risk.
examination.rationale: The ASA standard supports documenting previous anesthesia and medical therapy plus focused airway and physical-condition examination.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports previous-anesthesia history, medical therapy, focused examination, airway and physical-condition assessment, and factors affecting perioperative risk.

investigations.text: Supports clinician-selected tests or consultations, consent for anesthesia care, and recording the assessment in the medical record.
investigations.rationale: The standard supports recording clinician-selected tests, consultations, consent, and the completed assessment without generating them.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-selected tests or consultations, consent for anesthesia care, and recording the assessment in the medical record.

source_limitations: Procedure, urgency, complete medical and anesthesia history, medicines, allergies, fasting, functional status, airway findings, examination, tests, consultations, risk classification, plan, consent, and postoperative disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
